task_completion.description: Agent opens and reads the .onion address file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window displaying the contents of /home/ga/Desktop/securedrop_address.txt.

task_completion.description: Agent navigates to the SecureDrop .onion service.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Tor Browser (or another browser configured for Tor) open and displaying the SecureDrop web interface with a .onion URL in the address bar.

task_completion.description: Agent obtains the organization's PGP public key.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent viewing the PGP public key on the SecureDrop site, copying it, or importing it into a GPG keyring (e.g., via terminal or a GUI like Kleopatra) for the identity secure@drop.local.

task_completion.description: Agent encrypts the evidence file using the obtained PGP key.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal commands (e.g., gpg -e -a -r secure@drop.local /home/ga/Documents/evidence_tip.txt) or a GUI encryption tool actively encrypting the specific evidence_tip.txt file.

task_completion.description: Agent submits the ASCII-armored ciphertext through the SecureDrop form.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SecureDrop submission text area filled with an ASCII-armored PGP message (starting with '-----BEGIN PGP MESSAGE-----') and the agent clicking the submit button or showing a successful submission confirmation screen.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual SecureDrop web interface over Tor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar should show a .onion address, and the interface should be a genuine web page, not a local HTML file or text document.

integrity.description: Agent used actual GPG/PGP software to encrypt the file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of GPG terminal commands or a legitimate encryption GUI being used, rather than the agent hallucinating or pasting a fake PGP block directly from an LLM output.

integrity.description: Agent did not submit the evidence in plaintext.
integrity.visual_evidence: The submission box must contain a PGP ciphertext block, not the readable plaintext contents of the evidence_tip.txt file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The evidence file is located at /home/ga/Documents/evidence_tip.txt. The SecureDrop .onion URL is stored in /home/ga/Desktop/securedrop_address.txt. The target PGP key email to use for encryption is secure@drop.local.
